task_completion.description: Open the OpenC3 COSMOS web interface
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a web browser open to the OpenC3 COSMOS interface (typically showing a dashboard with tools like Command Sender, Telemetry Viewer, etc.).

task_completion.description: Observe the INST HEALTH_STATUS TEMP3 telemetry item
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the OpenC3 COSMOS interface displaying telemetry values for 'INST', 'HEALTH_STATUS', and 'TEMP3'. This is usually visible in the Telemetry Viewer, Packet Viewer, or Telemetry Grapher tools.

task_completion.description: Apply the calculated limits to the TEMP3 item in OpenC3 COSMOS
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for evidence of the agent setting or updating limits for the TEMP3 item within the OpenC3 COSMOS interface, such as using the Command Sender to send a limits update command or adjusting limits in a configuration screen.

task_completion.description: Create the limit calibration JSON report on the Desktop
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor (e.g., VS Code, gedit, nano) or file manager showing a file named 'limit_calibration_report.json' located in the /home/ga/Desktop/ directory.

task_completion.description: Verify the JSON report contains all required fields and at least 15 samples
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the contents of the JSON file in a text editor or terminal output. It must contain the keys: 'target' (INST), 'item' (TEMP3), 'sample_count' (value must be 15 or greater), 'raw_samples' (a list/array of numbers), 'baseline_min', 'baseline_max', and 'new_limits' (which must include red_low, yellow_low, yellow_high, red_high).

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the OpenC3 COSMOS GUI to view telemetry and set limits
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the agent actually opened and clicked through the OpenC3 COSMOS web interface tools (Telemetry Viewer, Command Sender, etc.) rather than bypassing the UI entirely with backend scripts.

integrity.description: Agent genuinely created and saved the JSON report file
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the JSON file was actually saved to the Desktop using a text editor or standard file writing commands, rather than just printing the JSON structure to the terminal without saving.

integrity.description: The limits in the JSON report appear to be mathematically derived from the raw samples
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the 'new_limits' values in the JSON file logically correspond to the 'baseline_min' and 'baseline_max' values shown, rather than being arbitrary, hallucinated, or hardcoded numbers.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target is 'INST', Item is 'TEMP3'. The output file must be saved to '/home/ga/Desktop/limit_calibration_report.json'. The margin multipliers are 0.2 for Yellow limits and 0.4 for Red limits. If the operational span (baseline_max - baseline_min) is exactly 0.0, a fallback span of 5.0 must be used for the calculations. The agent must collect a minimum of 15 samples.
